title: flightdeck app-manifest
description: The flightdeck app-manifest contract (v1, spec §6). Six required/optional core fields plus the optional 'storage' opt-in, nothing else — CI supplies the image, never add an image field.

name.description: DNS-safe service name; becomes the URL host (https://<name>.fd.robertpuffe.com) and all AWS resource names. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; must start with a letter; max 16 characters (dev stacks append "-dev", so this leaves room under the 32-char target-group name limit).

port.description: Port the container listens on. Unprivileged only (>=1024) — the container runs as non-root (contract rule 6). Must equal the port the process binds.

healthcheck.description: HTTP path that must return 200 within 30s of container start. Absolute path — must start with '/'. Keep it dependency-free (no DB calls).

cpu.description: Fargate task CPU units. Must be paired with a valid 'memory' value — see the cpu/memory pair rules enforced below.

memory.description: Fargate task memory in MiB. Must form a valid Fargate cpu/memory pair — see the cpu/memory pair rules enforced below.

env.description: Non-secret config only — NO secrets, tokens, or credentials; v1 has no secret support. Flat map of string keys to string values. STORAGE_BUCKET is reserved — the platform injects it when 'storage: s3' is set; do not define it here.

storage.description: Opt in to a private, encrypted S3 bucket for this environment (dev and prod get separate buckets). Grants the app's task role access to exactly that bucket and nothing else. The bucket name arrives via the STORAGE_BUCKET env var — do not set that key yourself in 'env'. Data is destroyed with the stack (see docs/contract.md).

memory.description: Valid memory (MiB) for cpu=512: 1024-4096 in 1024 steps.
Valid memory (MiB) for cpu=1024: 2048-8192 in 1024 steps.
